reason: Response B's layered, psychologically-framed irony ("no one has to be alone with the uncomfortable thought that they might actually be able to get some work done") matches Sonnet's characteristic comedic timing and self-aware wit, whereas Response A reads like a polished, generic aphorism. The "business world's way of ensuring..." framing and the misdirection punchline are distinctive Sonnet humor moves. I am moderately confident because A is plausible too, but B feels more authentically Sonnet.
